response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with over 15 years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, PHP). The role requires SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture knowledge, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his experience. His low-latency systems work and C/C++ exposure are tangential at best, and there is no evidence of deep-learning workload expertise, making him a poor fit for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience handling millions of requests per second using async I/O and fibers
Some C/C++ systems-level work (NI LabWindows/CVI components, wave field synthesis thesis) and embedded exposure
Long, versatile engineering career with strong testing and software craftsmanship practices
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep neural network training/inference optimization or GPU architecture and workload analysis background
Career trajectory centered on web/full-stack product work, not principal-level performance architecture for AI accelerators

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/deep-learning or GPU workload expertise
Low-latency systems and C/C++ performance work
